capability.name: State Management
capability.description: Choose appropriate state management patterns for given scenarios. Distinguish local vs. shared vs. server state. Select the right tool (useState, context, Zustand, server cache, URL state) for each category of state. Avoid both under-engineering and over-engineering.

templates.promptTemplate:
You're building: {{application}}

Here are the different kinds of state in play:
{{state_categories}}

For each category of state:
1. Where should it live? (component local, lifted to parent, context, external store, URL, server cache)
2. What tool/pattern would you use? Be specific (not just 'a state manager').
3. Why that choice over the alternatives?
4. What would be the sign that you chose wrong — what symptom would tell you to reconsider?

sets.application: a project management app (like a simplified Jira)
sets.state_categories:
1. Current user session/auth
2. List of projects and their boards
3. Currently selected project and board filters
4. Board column order (user can drag to rearrange)
5. Modal open/close state (create task, edit task)
6. Form state for task creation (title, description, assignee, etc.)
7. Real-time updates when other users modify the board

sets.application: an e-commerce product catalog with search, filters, and a shopping cart
sets.state_categories:
1. Product catalog data (fetched from API, paginated)
2. Active search query and filters (category, price range, sort)
3. Shopping cart contents
4. Product detail view (selected product)
5. Recently viewed products
6. Checkout form state

criteria.description: Placed each type of state at the right level (local vs. shared vs. server vs. URL)
Chose appropriate specific tools for each state category
Identified symptoms of choosing the wrong approach

templates.promptTemplate:
A developer proposes the following state management setup for {{application}}:

{{proposed_setup}}

The app currently has {{user_count}} users and {{dev_count}} developers.

Is this state management approach appropriate, over-engineered, or under-engineered? Be specific about what's right, what's overkill, and what's missing.

sets.application: an internal admin dashboard for a startup
sets.proposed_setup: Redux Toolkit with RTK Query for all data fetching, Redux Saga for side effects, normalized entity adapters for every data type, a hand-rolled middleware for logging, and Reselect for every derived value. Full test coverage of reducers and selectors.

sets.application: a real-time trading dashboard showing live market data
sets.proposed_setup: useState for everything. Each widget fetches its own data with useEffect + fetch. No shared state — components are 'independent'. Data refreshes on a 5-second interval with setInterval in each component.

criteria.description: Correctly assessed whether the approach is over or under-engineered
Identified what (if anything) is appropriate in the proposal
Proposed a more appropriate approach for the actual scale
